seoTitle: Novosense Solutions | Isolated Power and Signal Chain Applications | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Novosense application solutions for industrial automation, automotive electronics, power supplies, and green energy. BeiLuo provides technical support and reference designs for Novosense isolation and signal chain products.

faqs.question: What application solutions does Novosense provide?
faqs.answer: Novosense provides comprehensive application solutions leveraging their isolation and signal chain product portfolio: Industrial Motor Control Solutions featuring isolated gate drivers for IGBT and SiC MOSFET applications, digital isolators for PWM and feedback signals, and current sense amplifiers for motor current monitoring. Automotive Battery Management Solutions utilizing isolated RS-485 transceivers for BMS communication, digital isolators for cell monitoring isolation, and current sense amplifiers for pack current measurement. Solar Inverter Solutions incorporating isolated gate drivers for power stage switching, isolated transceivers for grid communication, and signal chain products for voltage and current sensing. Each solution includes reference designs, BOM recommendations, and application notes to accelerate customer development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse the solutions below to find the application matching your requirements. Contact BeiLuo FAE for customized solution recommendations and reference design support.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Novosense solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Novosense solution requires understanding your system requirements: For motor control applications, identify your motor type (AC induction, PMSM, BLDC), power level, and switching frequency to determine gate driver requirements. For battery management, determine your battery voltage (48V, 400V, 800V), number of cells, and communication requirements. For solar inverters, identify your DC bus voltage, AC output voltage, and power rating. For industrial communication, determine your network topology, data rate, and number of nodes. Novosense solutions are designed to address these specific requirements with optimized product combinations. BeiLuo FAE team can provide detailed solution selection guidance based on your specific application parameters and help with system architecture decisions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your application requirements including voltage, current, power level, and communication needs. Review the solution details below or contact BeiLuo FAE for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What reference designs are available for Novosense solutions?
faqs.answer: Novosense provides comprehensive reference designs for each major solution category: Motor Control Reference Designs include complete schematics, PCB layouts, and BOMs for 3-phase inverters ranging from 1kW to 100kW. These designs demonstrate proper gate driver implementation, current sensing circuits, and isolated communication interfaces. Battery Management Reference Designs showcase cell monitoring isolation, pack current measurement, and BMS communication networks with complete documentation. Solar Inverter Reference Designs feature power stage gate drive circuits, grid-tie communication, and MPPT sensing implementations. All reference designs include application notes explaining design choices, thermal considerations, and EMC optimization techniques. BeiLuo provides these reference designs with technical support to help customers adapt them to their specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from BeiLuo for your target application. Our FAE team can provide design review and customization support to adapt reference designs to your specific requirements.

faqs.question: Does Novosense provide automotive-qualified solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Novosense offers AEC-Q100 qualified solutions specifically designed for automotive applications: EV Battery Management Solutions using NSi1050-Q1 isolated RS-485 for BMS communication, NSi8220-Q1 digital isolators for cell monitoring isolation, and NSC2860-Q1 current sense amplifiers for pack current measurement. On-Board Charger (OBC) Solutions featuring NSi6602-Q1 isolated gate drivers for power factor correction and DC-DC stages. Motor Drive Solutions for electric vehicle traction motors using automotive-grade isolated gate drivers and current sensing. All automotive solutions include AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified components, PPAP documentation support, and automotive-specific application notes addressing functional safety and EMC requirements. BeiLuo provides full support for automotive qualification including PPAP documentation and technical consultation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, specify -Q1 qualified products. Contact BeiLuo for automotive solution recommendations and PPAP documentation support.

faqs.question: What technical support does BeiLuo provide for Novosense solutions?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Novosense core distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support for solution implementation: Application Engineering Support includes schematic review, PCB layout guidance, and thermal analysis for your specific design. Reference Design Customization to adapt standard solutions to your unique requirements including component selection and BOM optimization. SPICE Model Support for circuit simulation and verification before hardware implementation. EMC Design Review to ensure your design meets industrial and automotive EMC requirements. On-Site Engineering Support for critical projects requiring hands-on assistance. Training and Workshops on Novosense products and applications for your engineering team. BeiLuo FAE team has deep expertise in isolation technology and power electronics, enabling us to provide practical guidance for challenging design requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Engage BeiLuo FAE team early in your design cycle for maximum support value. We can provide design review, reference designs, and ongoing technical consultation throughout your development process.

faeInsights.insight: The key to successful motor control design with Novosense products is proper attention to isolation barrier integrity and CMTI performance. In my experience supporting industrial motor drive customers, the NSi6230 and NSi6602 gate drivers provide excellent performance when implemented with proper PCB layout practices. The 150kV/us CMTI rating is critical for modern IGBT and SiC applications where dv/dt can exceed 50kV/us. I always recommend using the integrated Miller clamp feature in half-bridge configurations to prevent false turn-on, which has eliminated shoot-through failures in customer designs. For current sensing, the NSC2860's 100V common-mode range simplifies high-side current measurement without complex isolated amplifiers. The reference designs from Novosense provide an excellent starting point, but attention to thermal design and EMC layout is essential for reliable field performance.
faeInsights.logic: Motor control design follows this decision framework: First, determine motor power and switching frequency to select appropriate gate driver (NSi6230 for <50kHz, NSi6602 for high-frequency SiC). Second, calculate required isolation voltage based on DC bus voltage (5kVrms for 480V systems). Third, implement proper PCB layout with attention to creepage distances and ground plane design. Fourth, add current sensing with NSC2860 for overcurrent protection and control feedback. Fifth, implement isolated communication with NSi1050 for industrial network connectivity.
keyTakeaways: Select gate driver based on switching frequency and power device type
Use Miller clamp in half-bridge configurations to prevent shoot-through
Implement proper PCB layout for isolation and EMC performance
Use NSC2860 for simple high-side current sensing
Verify CMTI margin for your specific dv/dt requirements
commonPitfalls: Insufficient creepage distance on PCB causing isolation failure
Inadequate gate resistor selection causing oscillation or slow switching
Poor ground plane design causing EMI issues
Missing Miller clamp in half-bridge leading to shoot-through
Inadequate thermal design for gate driver power dissipation
bestPractices: Follow reference design PCB layout guidelines closely
Use 120 ohm termination on RS-485 networks
Implement proper bypassing with 0.1uF and 1uF capacitors
Add external TVS protection for severe EMC environments
Verify isolation barrier integrity with hipot testing
faeInsights.content: [Data Pending] FAE insights to be added based on actual application experience with this solution.

resources.title: Motor Control Gate Driver Design Guide

faqs.question: What motor types are supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: The Novosense motor control solution supports various motor types through appropriate control algorithm implementation: AC Induction Motors (ACIM) using V/Hz control or field-oriented control (FOC) with the isolated gate drivers providing robust switching. Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) including surface-mounted and interior permanent magnet types using FOC with current feedback from NSC2860 sense amplifiers. Brushless DC Motors (BLDC) using trapezoidal commutation with Hall sensor or sensorless control. The solution provides the isolation and sensing infrastructure; the specific motor control algorithm is implemented in the MCU. For servo applications requiring precise position control, the isolated communication interface enables connection to encoders and industrial networks.
faqs.decisionGuide: This solution provides isolation and sensing for any motor type. Select your MCU and implement appropriate control algorithm (V/Hz, FOC, or BLDC). Contact BeiLuo for specific motor control recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I implement overcurrent protection in this motor control solution?
faqs.answer: Overcurrent protection is implemented using the NSC2860 current sense amplifiers and external comparators: The NSC2860 monitors motor phase currents with 3% accuracy and 400kHz bandwidth. Output is fed to fast comparators (response time <1us) with adjustable thresholds. When overcurrent is detected, the comparator output triggers immediate shutdown through the gate driver disable pins. Hardware protection operates independently of software for fastest response. Software monitoring provides additional protection with programmable thresholds and fault logging. For short-circuit protection, desaturation detection can be added to the gate drivers for IGBT protection. I recommend implementing both hardware fast protection (<5us response) and software protection (<100us response) for comprehensive fault coverage. The reference design includes example protection circuits and recommended component values.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement hardware overcurrent protection using NSC2860 and fast comparators for <5us response. Add software monitoring for programmable thresholds and diagnostics. Follow reference design protection circuit examples.

faqs.question: What are the EMC considerations for this motor control solution?
faqs.answer: EMC design is critical for reliable motor control operation: The Novosense gate drivers provide high CMTI (>150kV/us) to prevent false triggering from switching transients. Isolation barriers naturally attenuate common-mode noise between control and power stages. For conducted emissions, proper input filtering on the DC bus is essential. For radiated emissions, shielded cables for motor connections and proper PCB layout with ground planes are recommended. The reference design includes EMC-optimized PCB layout with proper creepage distances, ground plane segmentation, and filtering. For severe industrial environments, additional external filtering may be needed on power and communication lines. All Novosense products are tested to industrial EMC standards including IEC 61800-3 for motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference design PCB layout for EMC optimization. Add external filters for severe environments. Test EMC performance early in development. Contact BeiLuo FAE for EMC design review.

faqs.question: What is the typical development timeline for implementing this Industrial Motor Control Solution?
faqs.answer: Development timeline varies based on project complexity. Typical implementation takes 2-4 weeks for evaluation and prototyping, followed by 4-8 weeks for integration and testing. BeiLuo provides technical support throughout the development process to help meet your timeline. Reference designs can significantly accelerate development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan 2-4 weeks for evaluation, 4-8 weeks for integration. Use reference designs to accelerate development. Contact BeiLuo for project planning assistance.

faqs.question: What technical support does BeiLuo provide for this Industrial Motor Control Solution?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support including: Application engineering assistance for design and integration, Reference design customization for your specific requirements, PCB layout review and optimization, EMC design guidance, On-site support for critical projects, Training and workshops. Our FAE team has deep expertise in Novosense products and can help ensure successful implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Engage BeiLuo FAE team early in your project for maximum support value. We provide support from concept through production.

solutions.title: Industrial Motor Control Solution
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Motor Control Solution from novosense represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of novosense components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.

Comprehensive technical support from BeiLuo FAE team

faeInsights.insight: Automotive BMS design requires careful attention to functional safety and long-term reliability. In my experience supporting EV battery manufacturers, the Novosense AEC-Q100 qualified products provide the reliability and documentation needed for automotive production. The key design considerations are: Isolation voltage must provide adequate margin for the maximum battery voltage including transients - for 400V systems, 5kVrms reinforced isolation provides >10x safety margin. Communication reliability is critical for BMS operation, and the NSi1050-Q1's integrated fail-safe ensures defined behavior during fault conditions. Current sensing accuracy directly affects state-of-charge calculation, so the NSC2860-Q1's 3% accuracy over temperature is essential. For high-voltage systems, proper PCB creepage and clearance distances per IEC 60664-1 are mandatory. The reference designs include automotive-specific considerations including thermal management and vibration resistance.
faeInsights.logic: BMS design follows this framework: First, determine battery voltage and architecture (centralized vs distributed). Second, select isolation components with adequate voltage rating and AEC-Q100 qualification. Third, implement communication network with proper termination and fail-safe features. Fourth, design current sensing with appropriate shunt resistor and accuracy requirements. Fifth, ensure PCB layout meets automotive creepage and clearance requirements. Sixth, implement diagnostic and fault detection for functional safety compliance.
keyTakeaways: Use AEC-Q100 qualified components for automotive applications
Ensure isolation voltage provides 10x margin over battery voltage
Implement fail-safe communication for reliable BMS operation
Design current sensing for required SOC accuracy
Follow automotive PCB layout guidelines for creepage and clearance
commonPitfalls: Insufficient isolation voltage margin for high-voltage transients
Inadequate communication fail-safe leading to undefined states
Poor PCB layout causing isolation failure over time
Inadequate current sense accuracy affecting SOC calculation
Missing diagnostic coverage for functional safety requirements
bestPractices: Use reinforced isolation (5kVrms) for battery systems
Implement redundant communication paths for critical functions
Add external TVS protection for severe transient environments
Follow ISO 26262 guidelines for functional safety
Perform thorough environmental testing including thermal cycling

resources.title: Functional Safety in Battery Management Systems

faqs.question: What battery voltages are supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: The Novosense BMS solution supports battery systems from 48V mild hybrid to 800V full electric vehicle applications: 48V systems use standard isolation (3.75kVrms) with comfortable safety margins. 400V EV systems require reinforced isolation (5kVrms) to provide adequate margin over nominal voltage plus transients. 800V systems also use 5kVrms isolation but require careful attention to PCB creepage and clearance distances. The current sense amplifiers support up to 100V common-mode voltage, suitable for pack-level current measurement in all battery configurations. For cell-level monitoring, the digital isolators provide isolation between cell monitoring modules and the main BMS controller. The solution scales from small 48V systems to large grid-scale storage by adjusting the number of isolated communication nodes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select isolation rating based on battery voltage: 3.75kVrms for 48V systems, 5kVrms for 400V and 800V systems. Verify PCB layout meets creepage requirements for your specific voltage. Contact BeiLuo for high-voltage design support.

faqs.question: How does the solution meet automotive functional safety requirements?
faqs.answer: The Novosense automotive BMS solution supports functional safety implementation: AEC-Q100 qualified components provide the reliability foundation for ASIL-rated systems. The isolated communication includes diagnostic features for fault detection including open/short detection on bus lines. The current sense amplifiers provide accurate measurement for state-of-charge calculation, which is critical for safe battery operation. The 5kVrms isolation provides robust galvanic separation between high-voltage battery and low-voltage control systems. For ASIL compliance, the system must implement additional safety mechanisms including redundant measurements, diagnostic coverage, and safe state management. Novosense provides FMEDA (Failure Modes, Effects, and Diagnostic Analysis) documentation to support safety case development. BeiLuo FAE team can assist with functional safety architecture design and safety analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use AEC-Q100 qualified Novosense components as the foundation for ASIL-rated BMS. Implement additional safety mechanisms per ISO 26262. Request FMEDA documentation from BeiLuo for safety analysis.

faqs.question: What is the recommended communication topology for BMS applications?
faqs.answer: The recommended BMS communication topology depends on battery architecture: For centralized BMS with all cell monitoring in one module, use isolated RS-485 (NSi1050) for communication to external systems and digital isolators (NSi8220) for internal isolation. For distributed BMS with multiple cell monitoring modules, use isolated RS-485 in a multi-drop configuration with each module as a node. The NSi1050 supports up to 256 nodes, sufficient for large battery packs. For vehicle integration, use isolated CAN (NSi81C85) for communication with vehicle controllers. The recommended architecture uses RS-485 for internal BMS communication between modules and CAN for external vehicle communication. Termination is critical - use 120 ohm termination at both ends of each bus segment. For very large systems, use repeaters to extend the network.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RS-485 multi-drop for internal BMS module communication, CAN for vehicle integration. Implement proper termination and consider network segmentation for large systems. Follow reference design communication architecture.

solutions.description: Complete isolation solution for residential and commercial solar inverters, featuring isolated gate drivers for power stage switching, isolated transceivers for grid communication, and signal chain products for precise voltage and current sensing.
features: Isolated gate drivers for IGBT and SiC MOSFET power stages
Isolated RS-485/CAN transceivers for grid monitoring
Precision current sense amplifiers for MPPT control
Digital isolators for control signal isolation
Reference design for 5kW to 50kW solar inverters

Isolated CAN transceiver for grid communication
High-precision current sense amplifier for MPPT

benefits: High-efficiency power conversion with SiC support
Reliable grid communication with isolated transceivers
Precise MPPT control with high-accuracy sensing

customerCases.challenge: Needed high-efficiency isolation solution for 10kW residential solar inverter with SiC power stage
customerCases.solution: Implemented NSi6602 gate drivers and NSi1050 CAN transceivers with complete Novosense solution
customerCases.result: Achieved 98.5% peak efficiency and passed grid-tie certification on first attempt

faeInsights.content: Solar inverter designs require careful attention to isolation and switching performance. The NSi6602 gate driver is particularly well-suited for SiC MOSFET applications with its high CMTI and fast propagation delay. For grid-tie applications, the reinforced isolation rating of 5kVrms provides necessary safety margins. I always recommend implementing active Miller clamping for high-power SiC designs to prevent false turn-on. The NSC2860 current sense amplifier provides excellent accuracy for MPPT control, which directly impacts system efficiency. When designing the isolation system, pay close attention to creepage and clearance distances to meet safety standards.
keyTakeaways: Use NSi6602 for SiC MOSFET gate drive
Implement active Miller clamp for high-power designs
High-accuracy sensing critical for MPPT efficiency

steps: Determine power level and switching frequency
Select gate driver based on power device type (IGBT vs SiC)
Choose isolation rating based on grid-tie requirements
Implement proper sensing for MPPT control

faqs.question: What gate driver is recommended for SiC MOSFET solar inverters?
faqs.answer: For SiC MOSFET solar inverters, I recommend the NSi6602 isolated gate driver. Key features: High peak output current of 4A source/6A sink for fast switching of SiC devices, High CMTI >150kV/us for reliable operation in high dv/dt environments, Fast propagation delay <50ns for precise timing control, Active Miller clamp to prevent false turn-on during fast switching, Desaturation protection for overcurrent fault detection, Wide output voltage range supporting various SiC device requirements. For 10kW+ inverters using large SiC modules, the NSi6602's high drive capability ensures fast switching transitions, minimizing switching losses. The integrated protection features reduce external component count and improve system reliability. Always implement proper gate resistor selection based on your specific SiC device requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NSi6602 for SiC MOSFET applications. Contact FAE for gate resistor optimization and switching performance analysis.
